title: Add photo gallery section and final Valentine proposal page with playful Yes/No interaction

requirements.text: Add a dedicated photo section to the Valentine landing experience with the section heading exactly: "face of my heart", and display the user-uploaded photos in that section.

quotes: I want a different section of photos and the heading of that section should be "face of my heart"

acceptanceCriteria: A new photos section exists in the Valentine UI flow and is reachable by normal progression (or clearly placed on the page) without breaking the existing intro/letter/message/final experience.
The photos section heading text matches exactly: "face of my heart".
The three uploaded images are rendered in the section in a responsive layout (mobile and desktop).
Images load from static frontend assets (not via backend calls).

requirements.text: Add the uploaded images as static frontend assets and reference them from the new photos section using stable paths.

quotes: The user has uploaded 3 image(s): Snapchat-374559267.jpg, Snapchat-2137308291.jpg, Snapchat-1819211754.jpg.

acceptanceCriteria: The following files exist under the frontend public static assets and are used by the UI: Snapchat-374559267.jpg, Snapchat-2137308291.jpg, Snapchat-1819211754.jpg.
No build-time or runtime errors occur due to missing image files.
Images are displayed with correct orientation/fit (no unintended stretching), and include basic accessibility attributes (e.g., non-empty alt text or intentionally empty alt if purely decorative).

requirements.text: Add a new final page/step at the end of the Valentine flow that displays the text exactly: "will you be my valentine my rasmalai😍🫶" with two options: Yes and No.

quotes: also add a page at the end in which will be written "will you be my valentine my rasmalai😍🫶" and bottom of it there should be 2 options yes and no

acceptanceCriteria: After completing the existing flow, the user reaches a final proposal page/step.
The proposal text is displayed exactly as provided: "will you be my valentine my rasmalai😍🫶".

requirements.text: Make the "No" button effectively unpressable and implement a playful "run away" behavior when the user attempts to click/tap/hover it.

quotes: make the no button unpressable and if you tried to click it will run away

acceptanceCriteria: Attempting to interact with the "No" button (mouse hover, click, or touch) causes the button to move away so it cannot be easily pressed.
The "No" button does not trigger any navigation or success action.
The behavior works on both desktop (mouse) and mobile (touch) without causing layout breaks or scrolling the page unexpectedly.

requirements.text: When the user presses "Yes" on the proposal page, trigger a celebration theme with flowers falling from the top, hearts rising from the bottom, and show a popup message exactly: "I knew you love me so much🥰🫶".

quotes: if she press yes make a theme like flowers falling from the up and hearts rising from bottom and a pop up comes of "I knew you love me so much🥰🫶"

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking "Yes" triggers simultaneous animations: flowers falling from the top and hearts rising from the bottom.
A popup/modal appears containing exactly: "I knew you love me so much🥰🫶".
The celebration visuals do not block the popup readability and can be dismissed or naturally conclude without requiring a page refresh.
The interaction does not break the existing restart/share functionality elsewhere in the app (if still present).

constraints: Frontend must not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui or other immutablePaths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT; compose those components instead if needed.
Do not introduce new backend dependencies; backend/main.mo can remain unchanged unless strictly necessary.
Keep the app as a single React app (no Next.js, no external databases, no third-party auth beyond existing Internet Identity hooks).
All user-facing strings specified by the user must match exactly as provided (including emojis and casing).
nonGoals: Adding user accounts, admin panels, or saving photo uploads to canister storage.
Integrating third-party analytics, social APIs, or external hosting services.
Building multi-page routing beyond what is necessary to present the final proposal page/step within the existing flow.
